title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a policy document that seeks to establish guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence. This act aims to ensure that AI technology is used responsibly and for the defense of the country.

title: Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Heat Emergency Assessment and Tracking using AI Act aims to develop and implement AI-powered systems to monitor and track heat-related emergencies. This act will likely provide guidelines for the use of AI in managing heat emergencies, ensuring timely responses and relief.

title: To establish a pilot program for use by U.S. Customs and Border Protection at land ports of entry along the Arizona border to assess the use of artificial intelligence through an anomaly detection algorithm, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Homeland Security.
summary: The U.S. Customs and Border Protection plans to test the use of artificial intelligence at some of its Arizona border crossings to identify unusual activities. The program aims to assess the effectiveness of this technology in enhancing security at land ports of entry.

title: Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Small AI Innovators Empowerment Act appears to be a legislative initiative aimed at supporting and empowering small businesses or individuals in the field of artificial intelligence. Exact details of the act are not specified, but the title suggests it aims to promote innovation and growth in this area.

title: CREATE AI Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The CREATE AI Act of 2025 is a policy document that outlines guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States. The exact details of the policy are not available due to the lack of description, but it appears to be a regulatory framework for AI.

title: Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Rules and Administration.
summary: The Fraudulent Artificial Intelligence Regulations (FAIR) Elections Act of 2026 is a law aimed at regulating the use of artificial intelligence in elections to prevent fraudulent activities. It likely establishes rules to ensure the integrity and transparency of AI-powered processes in electoral systems.

title: Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
summary: The Department of Advanced Technology and Artificial Intelligence Act is a policy that likely establishes a government department responsible for the development and regulation of advanced technologies and artificial intelligence.

title: To amend the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 to require the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy to establish a prize competition program relating to artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: This bill aims to amend the 2020 National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act by adding a prize competition program for artificial intelligence, and also making other changes. The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy would oversee this new program.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act aims to put a temporary hold on the development of new artificial intelligence data centers. The exact specifics and scope of the moratorium are not available in the provided information.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is a proposed law that aims to study and mitigate the negative environmental effects of artificial intelligence systems. Its purpose is to protect the environment from the growing influence of AI technology.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: The SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026 is a law that likely aims to guide the use of artificial intelligence systems within the Small Business Administration (SBA). The specifics of this act are currently unknown due to the lack of a detailed description.

title: American Leadership in AI Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Energy and Commerce, Agriculture, Oversight and Government Reform, Education and Workforce, the Judiciary, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary:
The American Leadership in AI Act aims to advance the development and use of artificial intelligence in the United States, while also addressing concerns around its ethics and governance. 

This act provides a framework for the United States to maintain a competitive edge in the field of AI, while also ensuring responsible AI development and use.

title: Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Voluntary Consumer AI Disclosure Pilot Act is a proposed law that aims to inform consumers about the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in consumer transactions. It appears to be a voluntary effort to increase transparency about AI, rather than a mandatory requirement.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: The Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act aims to shield families from increased energy costs generated by the growing demand of artificial intelligence data centers. This act seeks to mitigate the financial burden of rising energy expenses on consumers.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Environment and Public Works.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address and mitigate the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence. The Act seeks to establish guidelines and regulations for AI development to ensure its eco-friendly and sustainable advancement.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 is a policy document that establishes a framework for regulating artificial intelligence across different sectors. This document likely outlines specific guidelines and regulations to ensure the safe and responsible development and use of AI technologies within various industries.

title: Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary:
The Algorithmic Accountability Act of 2025 is a policy that holds accountable algorithms and technology companies for the decisions made by their systems. 
By implementing regulations, this act aims to ensure that algorithms are transparent, fair, and do not discriminate against individuals or groups.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 aims to manage and regulate artificial intelligence risks at the federal level. This act is expected to set guidelines and standards for the development, deployment, and oversight of AI systems.

title: A bill to address the effects of artificial intelligence-enabled systems, including artificial intelligence chatbots, on older adults, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This document proposes a bill to address the effects of artificial intelligence on older adults and includes provisions to address chatbots and other purposes. The bill aims to create policies that mitigate potential negative impacts of AI on older adults.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to create a framework that allows the development and use of artificial intelligence technology in financial services. This act likely seeks to promote innovation, growth, and competition in the financial sector through the integration of AI.

title: China AI Power Report Act
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 47 - 0.
summary: The China AI Power Report Act is an unnamed policy document that appears to be related to the regulation of artificial intelligence in China.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The READ AI Models Act appears to be a policy document, but without further information, I can only provide a generic summary based on the title: This act likely relates to regulating or setting standards for artificial intelligence (AI) models, and the purpose of the act is to establish guidelines or rules that must be followed by organizations using these models.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Workforce for AI Trust Act is a policy document that aims to govern the development and implementation of artificial intelligence technology. However, without a detailed description, the specific contents and goals of the Act are unclear.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
summary: The K-12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 aims to prepare school-age children by teaching them about Artificial Intelligence. This Act likely seeks to incorporate AI education into the school curriculum.

title: Preventing AI Censorship Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: The Preventing AI Censorship Act aims to protect freedom of speech online by preventing AI systems from censoring or removing content. The policy seeks to promote a balanced approach between AI moderation and individual online expression.

title: Generative AI Terrorism Risk Assessment Act
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This act aims to assess and mitigate the risk of terrorism posed by generative artificial intelligence systems. It likely outlines measures and regulations to detect and prevent potential threats from AI-generated content.

title: A bill to prohibit certain uses of automated decision systems by employers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
summary: This policy document aims to restrict employers from using automated decision systems in certain ways. It appears to be a proposed law intended to regulate the use of AI by employers for employment purposes.

title: A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to impose an excise tax on systemically important AI activity, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to introduce an excise tax on certain significant artificial intelligence activities, with a focus on their impact on the system of the US. The bill's purpose is also to address other matters related to artificial intelligence.

title: Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Secure and Accountable Military AI Act of 2026 is a proposed law to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in the military. It likely aims to ensure AI systems used by the military are secure and transparent, while also being accountable for their actions.
